text_content: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IITH) Inventing and Innovating in Technology for Humanity (IITH) Announcements ГўВ—ВҸ High Entropy Alloys: Open to All Teaching Course (Starts 23 March 2026) ГўВ—ВҸ Phd Admissions for July 2026 opening shortly ГўВ—ВҸ PG Admissions for July 2026 opening shortly ГўВ—ВҸ Open to All Teaching ГўВ—ВҸ Tenders & Notices What's Happening at IITH Upcoming Events Conferences, symposiums, and flagship events that bring innovation, research, and culture together. 27 Mar to 28 Mar Conference on Data-driven Statistical Decision Making View Details ГўВҶВ’ 06 Jul to 08 Jul 4th International Conference on Recent Advances in Applied Mathematics View Details ГўВҶВ’ View All Events ГўВҶВ’ Media & Outreach Research Activities India's First 3D Printed Bridge IIT Hyderabad Campus Innovating Infrastructure India's First 3D Printed Bridge at IIT Hyderabad. Preserving India's Cultural Heritage: Inside the Digital Heritage Lab at IIT Hyderabad The Digital Heritage Lab at IIT Hyderabad, established six years ago, is at the forefront of preserving IndiaГўВҖВҷs cultural heritage through innovative technology. Powering the Future of Semiconductors IIT Hyderabad is steadily emerging as a hub for semiconductor research and training, with innovations ranging from advanced image sensors to next-generation chips. National Ranking 7 NIRF ГўВҖВ“ Engineering QS Ranking 270 QS Asian University Ranking 2026 Student Community 5691 Students Enrolled UG ГўВҖВў PG ГўВҖВў PhD Research Strength 1576 PhD Scholars Alumni Impact 8615 Graduates Across the globe 713+ Patents Filed 339+ Startups Supported and generate a revenue of Rs. 1659+ Cr (Rs. 335+ Cr funding in 2024-25, i.e. Rs. 1+ Cr per Faculty, one of the largest per capita funding among IITs) 5266+ R & D Projects worth of Rs. 1640+ Cr Research Focus Research & Centres of Excellence Interdisciplinary centres driving breakthrough research, strategic partnerships, and advanced technology development. CoE VIGRAHA DBT-SAHAJ National Facility DST SATHI ГўВҖВ“ CISCoM IITH-Nikon Centre of Excellence TRI HUB - Transportation Research and Innovation Hub TiHAN - Technology Innovation Hub Explore All Research Centres ГўВҶВ’ A Message from the Director Leadership, vision, and the journey of IIT Hyderabad Prof. B S Murty Director, IIT Hyderabad Dear Friends, Welcome to Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IITH). ГўВҖВңInventing and Innovating in Technology for Humanity (IITH)ГўВҖВқ is more than just a motto for IIT Hyderabad; it is the philosophy that defines our identity. As an institute, we are committed to pushing the frontiers of knowledge while ensuring that our ideas and innovations directly benefit people, industries, and the nation. We are proud of preparing dynamic leaders who make a difference to the world, nurtured with the skills required for a better tomorrow. Though IITH has completed only 17 years of its existence, it is gratifying to note that we are already almost on par with some of the first-generation IITs, standing at the forefront of nurturing talent and innovation. The academic and research vibrancy of IITH is reflected in NIRF-2025 , where we achieved 7th rank among Engineering institutes , 6th in Innovation , 15th in Research , and 12th Overall . Internationally, IITH is ranked 664 in QS World University Rankings-2026 , placing us among the top 10% of global institutions in citations per faculty. With a student strength of over 5,750+ across UG, PG, and PhD programs (60% PG+PhD), we continue to lead in curriculum innovationГўВҖВ”introducing IndiaГўВҖВҷs first BTech in Artificial Intelligence . Our Open to All Teaching (OAT) initiative and newly launched recording studio strengthen our commitment to digital and inclusive education. At IITH, research and entrepreneurship go hand in hand. We have supported 320+ startups , launched the BUILD program, and recently introduced BHARATI , engaging 660 first-year students under 90 faculty mentors in hands-on engineering experiences. Our ambitious initiative, ГўВҖВңPatent a Day: Mission 365ГўВҖВқ , reflects our focus on translational research. IITH has filed 580+ patents so far, including 210+ in the last year alone . We now host 30+ Centres and Centres of Excellence supporting impactful research and industry collaboration. Internationally, we have established strategic partnerships across seven countries, including Japan, Australia, the USA, and EuropeГўВҖВ”reflecting our commitment to global excellence. Our alumni continue to inspire us through leadership in civil services, entrepreneurship, social impact, and philanthropy. As India advances through initiatives such as Digital India, Make in India, Startup India, Atmanirbhar Bharat , and the Amrit Kaal Vision 2047 , IITH proudly aligns its efforts with national priorities. Guided by the philosophy of ГўВҖВңSabka Saath, Sabka VikasГўВҖВқ , we actively contribute to inclusive and sustainable development, especially through our Rural Development Centre initiatives. Looking ahead to Viksit Bharat @2047 , we aspire to position IIT Hyderabad as a hub of deep-tech research, transformative education, and human-centred innovation. I invite all stakeholdersГўВҖВ”students, faculty, staff, researchers, industry partners, and alumniГўВҖВ”to join us in this exciting journey of national development and global impact. Prof. B S Murty Director, IIT Hyderabad Innovations @ IITH From Lab to Impact National Firsts in 3D Printing IITH, in collaboration with Simpliforge Creations, has delivered two national firsts, IndiaГўВҖВҷs first on-site 3D-printed protective structure in Leh with Lt Col Arun Krishnan & IndiaГўВҖВҷs first 3D-printed pedestrian bridge, conceptualized and evaluated by Prof K V L Subramaniam & his research group. Autonomous Campus Shuttle TiHAN at IITH has developed an autonomous campus shuttle, a driverless electric vehicle designed to operate within campus roads. Equipped with LiDAR, cameras, GPS, and AI-based navigation, it can detect obstacles, follow predefined routes, and ensure passenger safety with features like automatic braking. Advanced System-on-Chip for IoT and Satellite Communication Prof Kiran Kuchi of IIT Hyderabad, in collaboration with WiSig Networks, has developed an advanced System-on-Chip that enables reliable, low-power connectivity for the Internet of Things and next-generation satellite communication. The chip supports precise positioning through GNSS and IndiaГўВҖВҷs NavIC system and can intelligently process sensor data in real time. Designed to be compact and energy-efficient, it helps extend battery life in connected devices, highlighting IITHГўВҖВҷs contribution to building indigenous, future-ready communication technologies for global use. 6G Research and Innovations 6G research led by Prof Kiran Kuchi in collaboration with WiSig Networks is working on 6G innovations built on WiSigГўВҖВҷs open and programmable future network architecture, focusing on energy-efficient, AI-ready radio access networks with intelligent massive MIMO, advanced waveforms for next-generation frequency bands, integrated sensing and communication for applications such as UAV detection, post-quantum security solutions, and the development of broadband modem System-on-Chip technologies for future wireless networks. Synthetic Cornea Development Dr Viswanath Chinthapenta, Prof Ramji M, Dr Sayan Basu (LVPEI) are working in partnership with LV Prasad Eye Institute to study the sutureability of the natural cornea. These findings provide fundamental insights into key design criteria for a synthetic cornea. PURE Energy Ltd PURE Energy Ltd by Dr Nishanth Dongari & Rohit Vadera, is India's 1st All-In-One Energy Storage Products for Home & Commercial Segments. DIGIPIN IITH, Department of Posts, and NRSC (ISRO) Collaborated to Launch DIGIPIN: A National-Scale Geospatial Addressing System for India, developed by Dr Shashank Vatedka, Prof Soumya Jana & Dr Lakshmi Prasad Natarajan, Tarandeep Singh, a former MTech (AI) student. DIGIPIN TiHAN at IITH has developed Palyanka, a heavy-payload autonomous air ambulance drone capable of carrying up to 200 kg for rapid medical evacuation, disaster rescue and cargo transport. Designed to operate without human intervention, it offers lifesaving mobility in traffic-clogged cities and difficult terrains.
